headline: Sabah Road Fatalities Highest in Malaysia at 12.8 Rate
context: Sabah recorded 12.8 road fatalities per 100,000 population in 2025 the highest nationally and 60% above Malaysian average.

cards.big: Sabah road fatality rate of 12.8 per 100,000 is Malaysia worst
cards.sub: Poor road conditions and long hospital distances are major contributing factors

cards.big: 480 people died in road accidents in Sabah in 2025 up from 420 in 2023
cards.sub: 40% of fatal accidents occurred on unlit two-lane roads between 6pm and midnight

cards.big: Only 28% of Sabah road network meets JKR federal highway safety standards
cards.sub: Missing guardrails and no road markings characterize 1,800km of state roads

cards.big: Average emergency response in rural Sabah is 45 minutes versus 12 minutes in KL
cards.sub: 12 district hospitals lack trauma care requiring transfers to Kota Kinabalu

cards.big: Road fatality rates test whether infrastructure safety standards apply equally across states
12.8 road fatality rate — Malaysia's highest — reflects engineering deficits, not driver behaviour alone.
